title: Four Core Metric Patterns That Diagnose Every Meta Performance Issue
insight: Every Meta performance problem maps to one of four metric patterns: (1) Frequency 2+ and CPA rising → creative fatigue, need fresh TOF creatives with fresh angles. (2) CPM and CPA rising → audience saturation or poor engagement, need more engaging messaging and new audiences. (3) CPA rising and CVR dropping → ad-to-landing-page mismatch, fix landing page alignment with ad messaging. (4) CTR dropping and CPA rising → creative fatigue again, need fresh TOF creatives with fresh angles. Understanding which metrics are moving together tells you exactly where the problem is — creative, audience, or landing page.
raw_excerpt: It all comes down to the 4 core metrics on Meta. If you understand them and how they come to be, you know how to solve any performance issue in the account. 1. Frequency 2+ and CPA rising? -> You need fresh TOF ad creatives with fresh angles. 2. CPM and CPA rising? -> Find a more engaging way to deliver your message, and target new audiences. 3. CPA rising and CVR dropping? -> Your ads are misaligned with your landing page. 4. CTR dropping and CPA rising? -> You need fresh TOF ad creatives with fresh angles.
actionable_steps: When ROAS drops, check frequency first — if 2+ with rising CPA, it's creative fatigue
If CPM and CPA are both rising, the problem is audience saturation or poor ad engagement — test new audiences and messaging
If CPA rises while CVR drops, the issue is post-click — audit landing page alignment with your ad creative
If CTR drops with rising CPA, creatives are stale — launch new TOF concepts with fresh angles

title: ROAS Troubleshooting Decision Tree: CPM, CTR, AOV, and CR as Sequential Diagnostics
insight: When ROAS drops, diagnose sequentially: First check CPM — if rising, target cheaper audiences. If CPM is stable, check CTR — if dropping, refresh creatives. If CTR is stable, check AOV — if dropping, bundle higher value offers. If AOV is stable, check conversion rate — if dropping, fix or rebuild the landing page. Each branch isolates a different lever: audience (CPM), creative (CTR), offer (AOV), or landing page (CR). Work through them in order to avoid fixing the wrong thing.
raw_excerpt: ROAS down? -> CPM rising? -> Target cheaper audiences. CTR down? -> Refresh creatives. AOV down? -> Bundle higher value offers. CR down? -> Fix / build better landing page.
actionable_steps: When ROAS drops, start diagnosis with CPM — rising CPM means you're paying too much for impressions
If CPM is fine but CTR is dropping, the creative isn't resonating — refresh or launch new concepts
If CTR is fine but AOV is dropping, the offer needs work — test bundles or higher-value products
If AOV is fine but CR is dropping, the landing page is the bottleneck — rebuild or optimize it
